description: Replace the old Document step with the new Structure step, update PDF generation for type-specific templates, update the process stepper and workspace flow, refresh the landing page, and perform a comprehensive final cleanup after the refactoring.

userStories.title: New Structure step view (replaces DocumentView)
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a dedicated Structure step view that shows the full-width requirements editor with a PDF preview sidebar, so that I can review and refine my structured requirements before submitting.
acceptanceCriteria: Structure step shows full-width RequirementsPanel in editing mode on the left
PDF preview sidebar on the right shows current requirements document
Generate/Regenerate button triggers AI generation
Allow information gaps toggle updates draft state
Readiness indicators show per-item status (sufficient vs insufficient)
Lock/unlock individual items works (prevents AI regeneration)
Submit for Review button appears at bottom (only enabled when ready or gaps allowed)
ProcessStepper shows step type 'structure' when on this step
URL param ?step=structure activates this view
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes:
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/project-structure.md § Frontend structure — create new component at frontend/src/pages/ProjectWorkspace/StructureStepView.tsx
Design: docs/03-design/page-layouts.md § Structure Step Layout (does not exist — this is new post-refactoring), docs/03-design/component-specs.md § RequirementsPanel, PDF Preview
Files: frontend/src/pages/ProjectWorkspace/StructureStepView.tsx (new), frontend/src/pages/ProjectWorkspace/index.tsx (update step rendering logic), frontend/src/components/workspace/RequirementsPanel.tsx (reuse from M19), frontend/src/components/workspace/PDFPreview.tsx (check if exists, may need to create), frontend/src/i18n/locales/en.json, frontend/src/i18n/locales/de.json
Gotchas: RequirementsPanel was built in M19 Story 3.3 but was used in 40/60 split with chat — now it needs to be full-width in Structure step. PDF preview component may not exist yet — check codebase. Submit for Review button logic should match ReviewSection submission flow (check existing submit endpoint). Step type 'structure' is new — ProcessStepper currently uses 'document' as the type even though the label says 'Structure'.
Deviations: Spec does not provide a detailed wireframe for Structure step layout. Infer from milestone description: left side = full-width requirements editor (reuse RequirementsPanel), right side = PDF preview sidebar. Use 60/40 split (requirements 60%, PDF 40%).

data_model: requirements_document_drafts: structure jsonb, item_locks jsonb, allow_information_gaps boolean, readiness_evaluation jsonb
components: RequirementsPanel — displays hierarchical structure with editing (M19)
PDFPreview — embeds generated PDF (check if exists)
ProcessStepper — step navigation (will be updated in US-003)
existing_code: frontend/src/components/workspace/DocumentView.tsx: DocumentView() — old BRD editor, to be replaced

userStories.title: PDF generation — Type-specific templates
userStories.description: As a user, I want PDF generation to produce documents with templates that match my project type (Software: Epics/Stories format, Non-Software: Milestones/Packages format), so that the output document structure matches my requirements model.
acceptanceCriteria: Software projects generate 'Software Requirements Document' with Epics section
Each epic renders as a section with title, description, and user story table (ID, Title, Description, Acceptance Criteria, Priority)
Non-software projects generate 'Project Requirements Document' with Milestones section
Each milestone renders as a section with title, description, and work package table (ID, Title, Description, Deliverables, Dependencies)
PDF CSS template includes Gotham font, Commerz Real branding
proto/pdf.proto updated to accept project_type and structure JSON
Gateway BRD views renamed to requirements_document (API endpoints, serializers, models)
PDF preview rendering in frontend handles new structure
Typecheck passes
Backend lint (Ruff) passes
userStories.notes:
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/api-design.md § PDF generation (gRPC), docs/02-architecture/project-structure.md § PDF service Design: docs/03-design/component-specs.md § PDF Preview Files: services/pdf/generator/builder.py (update build_html, BrdContent dataclass), services/pdf/templates/brd_styles.css (may rename to requirements_styles.css), proto/pdf.proto (update GeneratePDF RPC), services/gateway/apps/brd/ (rename to requirements_document), services/gateway/apps/requirements_document/views.py, services/gateway/apps/requirements_document/serializers.py, services/gateway/apps/requirements_document/urls.py, services/gateway/apps/requirements_document/tests/, frontend/src/api/projects.ts (update PDF API calls), frontend/src/components/workspace/PDFPreview.tsx Gotchas: The old BRD model used 6 flat sections (section_title, section_short_description, etc.). New model uses hierarchical structure JSON. builder.py needs to iterate over structure and render tables. Each epic/milestone is a section, each story/package is a table row. CSS must support nested tables and hierarchical headings. Gateway BRD app exists at services/gateway/apps/brd/ — must rename to requirements_document and update all imports. Proto file change requires re-generating Python gRPC stubs (run make proto or equivalent). PDF service is stateless — no database migrations needed. Deviations: Spec does not provide exact HTML template format for hierarchical structure. Infer table layout: each epic/milestone as

Epic/Milestone Title

Description

...
. Story table columns: ID (UUID first 8 chars), Title, Description (As a... I want...), AC (bullet list), Priority (badge). Package table columns: ID, Title, Description, Deliverables (bullet list), Dependencies (comma-separated).

data_model: requirements_document_drafts: structure jsonb — list of epic/milestone objects
api_endpoints: POST /api/projects/:id/requirements/pdf/generate — generate PDF (gRPC to PDF service)
components: PDFPreview — displays embedded PDF or download button
existing_code: services/pdf/generator/builder.py: build_html() — builds HTML from BRD sections
services/pdf/generator/renderer.py: render_pdf() — converts HTML to PDF bytes

userStories.title: Update process stepper & workspace flow
userStories.description: As a user, I want the process stepper to show 'Define', 'Structure', 'Review' with correct step types and rendering logic, so that the workflow matches the new requirements assembly process.
acceptanceCriteria: ProcessStepper displays 'Define', 'Structure', 'Review' labels
Step types are 'define', 'structure', 'review' (not 'brainstorm', 'document', 'review')
Define step renders chat + RequirementsPanel (40/60 split, same as before)
Structure step renders StructureStepView (full-width editor + PDF preview from US-001)
Review step renders ReviewSection (same as before)
Gate logic: canAccessStructure checks if requirements exist, canAccessReview checks if submitted
URL param ?step= accepts 'define', 'structure', 'review'
Translations updated for step labels (de.json, en.json)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes:
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/project-structure.md § Frontend structure
Design: docs/03-design/page-layouts.md § Process Stepper
Files: frontend/src/components/workspace/ProcessStepper.tsx (update step type enum, labels), frontend/src/pages/ProjectWorkspace/index.tsx (update step rendering logic, gate checks), frontend/src/i18n/locales/en.json, frontend/src/i18n/locales/de.json
Gotchas: ProcessStepper.tsx line 12 currently defines step types as 'brainstorm' | 'document' | 'review'. Change to 'define' | 'structure' | 'review'. Labels already say 'Define' and 'Structure' (lines 37, 42) but the type is still 'brainstorm' and 'document' internally. This is just a type rename. Gate checks: canAccessDocument should become canAccessStructure, canAccessReview stays the same. ProjectWorkspace index.tsx renders based on activeStep — update the switch/conditional to check for 'define', 'structure', 'review' instead of old types. URL parsing function parseStep (line 35) needs to accept new types.
Deviations: None — this is a straightforward rename and logic update.

components: ProcessStepper — step navigation component (current file uses old types)
ProjectWorkspace — main workspace container (renders different views per step)
existing_code: frontend/src/pages/ProjectWorkspace/index.tsx: parseStep() (line 35) — validates step type from URL param
frontend/src/pages/ProjectWorkspace/index.tsx: step rendering logic — conditionally renders chat/document/review based on activeStep

userStories.title: Landing page & project list updates
userStories.description: As a user, I want the landing page to show clear section titles ('My Projects', 'Collaborating', etc.) and project type badges on cards, so that I can quickly identify project types.
acceptanceCriteria: HeroSection shows heading and 'New Project' button (opens NewProjectModal)
Section titles: 'My Projects', 'Collaborating', 'Pending Invitations', 'Trash'
ProjectCard shows project type badge ('Software' or 'Non-Software')
Type badge styling: small pill, text-xs, bg-muted, text-muted-foreground
Filter by project type works (if filter UI exists)
Translations updated for section titles and type badges (de.json, en.json)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes:

Files: frontend/src/pages/LandingPage/index.tsx, frontend/src/components/landing/HeroSection.tsx, frontend/src/components/landing/ProjectCard.tsx, frontend/src/i18n/locales/en.json, frontend/src/i18n/locales/de.json
Gotchas: NewProjectModal was created in M19 Story 1 (US-001). HeroSection should already have a 'New Project' button from M19 — check if it exists. If so, this story is just verifying the flow works and adding type badges to ProjectCard. ProjectCard shows state badge — add type badge next to it. Filter by type may not exist yet — if not, this is optional (milestone spec says 'Update filter hooks' but acceptance criteria says 'if filter UI exists'). Check codebase for existing filter UI.
Deviations: None — this is polish and UI updates.

ProjectCard — displays project info (needs type badge)

userStories.title: Final cleanup & orphan check
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to ensure no orphaned references to removed features exist in the codebase, so that the refactoring is complete and the codebase is clean.
acceptanceCriteria: Grep search for 'idea' (case-insensitive) in code returns only valid references (not old 'Idea' model/API/component names)
Grep search for 'brainstorm' returns only valid references (not old step type)
Grep search for 'board_agent', 'BoardNode', 'xyflow', 'ReactFlow' returns no results (board removed in M17)
Grep search for 'merge_synth', 'keyword_agent', 'similarity', 'deep_comparison' returns no results (merge/similarity removed in M17)
Grep search for 'brd_draft', 'BrdDraft', 'brd_version' (case-sensitive) returns no results (renamed to requirements_document in US-002)
All services start without errors (docker compose up)
Frontend builds without errors (npm run build)
TypeScript typecheck passes (npx tsc --noEmit)
Backend lint passes (ruff check services/)
All tests pass (pytest + vitest)
No unused imports in Python files (ruff reports none)
No unused translation keys in en.json/de.json
Documentation updated (README, architecture docs) to reflect 'Project' terminology
userStories.notes:
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/ — verify all docs use 'project' terminology
Files: All codebase files (grep search), README.md, docs/02-architecture/*.md, frontend/src/i18n/locales/*.json
Gotchas: Some valid 'idea' references may exist in comments, documentation, or old migration files. Focus on active code (exclude migrations, exclude commented code). 'Brainstorm' may appear in translations as old keys — remove unused keys. Board references should be completely gone after M17, but check for missed cleanup. BRD -> requirements_document rename in US-002 should catch most brd_draft references, but double-check. Test execution: run full test suite, not just the tests for this milestone. Services start check: docker compose -f docker-compose.test.yml up -d --build (from pipeline-config.json).
Deviations: This story is a verification task, not a feature implementation. No specific test IDs assigned because it's a manual check + existing test suite. If orphans are found, they must be removed before marking this story as passing.

existing_code: All Python files: check for old 'Idea' model imports, 'brd_draft' references
All TypeScript files: check for 'BoardNode', 'ReactFlow', 'brainstorm' step type
Migration files: exclude from grep (these are historical and should not be changed)
Translation files: check for unused keys (keys not referenced in code)
